title: Brayan Bello excels in bulk relief, but opener melts in Red Sox’ 4-3 loss to Guardians
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Tyler Samaniego allowed four runs in the first inning, resulting in a 4-3 Red Sox loss to the Guardians.
Brayan Bello excelled in relief, pitching seven scoreless innings with five strikeouts after Samaniego's difficult opener.
The Red Sox, now 10 games under .500, have lost six of their last seven games and remain last in the AL East.

title: Giannis Antetokounmpo praises TD Garden environment amid trade rumors
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Giannis Antetokounmpo praises Celtics fans and TD Garden atmosphere during a recent livestream despite ongoing trade rumors.
Boston Celtics are considered contenders for Antetokounmpo, though the Miami Heat reportedly have an edge in negotiations.
Speculation involves possible trade of Jaylen Brown for Antetokounmpo, with concerns about fit alongside Jayson Tatum.

title: Harvard ‘Senior Sunrise’ cut short by cops
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Police dispersed Harvard's Senior Sunrise on Weeks Footbridge due to crowd size and swimming attempts in Charles River.
Cambridge, Harvard University, and Massachusetts State Police responded to the early morning gathering of the Class of 2026.
The event followed Harvard's commencement, with no immediate reports of arrests or injuries from the incident.

title: 5 must-watch movies & TV shows streaming right now
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: 'Kill Bill: The Whole Bloody Affair' is now streaming on Peacock, presenting Quentin Tarantino's complete vision as a single movie.
'The Four Seasons' Season 2, starring Tina Fey and Will Forte, is streaming on Netflix, exploring post-tragedy group dynamics.
'Spider-Noir' starring Nicolas Cage is available on Prime Video, offering viewers a choice between color and black & white versions.

title: Red Sox predicted to draft 18-year-old third baseman in 2026 MLB Draft by ESPN
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Red Sox predicted to draft Bo Lowrance, 18, a third baseman from South Carolina, in ESPN's 2026 MLB mock draft.
Lowrance, a left-handed batter, committed to University of Virginia, is compared to MLB star Freddie Freeman by scouts.
Red Sox expected to pick Lowrance at No. 20 in the draft on July 11, according to analyst Kiley McDaniel.

title: Lowell father and daughter charged in alleged payroll tax scheme
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Lim Ou, 69, and Nalen Ou, 39, from Lowell, charged with conspiracy to evade payroll taxes.
The Ous allegedly concealed over $48 million in cash payroll from temporary staffing businesses.
Between 2019-2025, $57 million was cashed, leading to unpaid federal employment taxes exceeding $12 million.

title: Report: Christian Gonzalez unlikely to participate in OTAs under current Patriots contract
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Christian Gonzalez is unlikely to join Patriots OTAs without a contract extension, per NBC Sports Boston.
Gonzalez seeks a raise from his current $4 million salary, aiming for $32-35 million annually.
Patriots have cap flexibility after restructuring Mike Onwenu's contract, aiding potential Gonzalez extension.

title: Search underway for driver who led cops on a chase through Everett, Malden, Stoneham
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: A suspect wanted for drug-related warrants led police on a chase through Everett, Malden, and Stoneham.
The driver struck a State Police cruiser and nearly hit a Malden officer before abandoning the vehicle in Stoneham.
Authorities found the abandoned vehicle, and the search for the driver and passenger is ongoing.

title: Ex-Patriots HC Jerod Mayo has a new job away from football
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Jerod Mayo, former Patriots head coach, became managing director of Fifth Down Capital in February.
Fifth Down Capital, based in Needham, invests in technology, healthcare, and consumer/retail sectors.
Mayo, 40, previously held a business role as VP of Business Development at Optum from 2015-2018.

title: Mass. man reports beating Mount Everest speed climbing record
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Tyler Andrews of Concord, Massachusetts, claims to have set a new speed climbing record on Mount Everest with supplemental oxygen.
Andrews completed the climb in 9 hours and 55 minutes, potentially surpassing the previous record by over an hour.
The Nepali authorities have not yet verified his record-breaking ascent, which was tracked by GPS data.
